channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The US Christian population has declined for years. A new survey shows that drop leveling off
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The number of Americans who identify as Christian has declined steadily for years, but that drop shows signs of slowing, according to a new survey Wednesday from the Pew Research Center. The Religious Landscape Study finds 62% of U.S. adults call themselves Christians.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: South Korea's Yoon defends his martial law decree as impeachment ruling nears
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In a final statement at his impeachment trial, South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol defended his martial law decree that plunged the country into chaos as a bid to inform the public of the danger of the opposition-controlled parliament as he vowed Tuesday to push for politi
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US energy secretary touts nuclear power as tech sector's thirst for electricity grows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright says it’s critical that the nation be out in front when it comes to artificial intelligence, and that means having reliable and affordable sources of electricity to meet the growing demands of the technology sector.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: House GOP, after pulling budget resolution, brings it back for a vote
item.description: House GOP leaders brought their budget resolution to the floor for the vote, just minutes after pulling it at the last minute Tuesday evening as they struggled to win over a handful of conservative fiscal hawks who had threatened to tank the framework for President Trump's ambitious legislative agen
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Idaho prosecutors seek to limit Bryan Kohberger's alibi evidence at murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Idaho prosecutors say a man charged in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students shouldn't be allowed to offer an alibi defense unless he takes the stand himself during the murder trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scenes at US Capitol on war's anniversary reflect partisan divide on Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 100 House members at one point packed the steps of the U.S. Capitol to show their support for Ukraine following Russia's invasion three years ago, Republicans joining with Democrats in a forceful display of bipartisanship.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 21 DOGE staffers resign over Musk's demands
item.description: In a resignation letter, the tech workers said they wouldn't help "dismantle critical public services," as Elon Musk and DOGE try to cut the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, KTLA 5

item.title: He's had three 'last meals' on Oklahoma's death row. The Supreme Court has now tossed his conviction
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Richard Glossip has spent 27 years behind bars, most of it on Oklahoma's death row, coming close enough to execution that he has had nine separate execution dates and been fed three “last meals.” On Tuesday, Glossip, now 62, won a new trial in a stunning U.S.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man forced to sit next to corpse after woman died midway through flight
item.description: Passenger Mitchell Ring said the flight crew did everything they could to revive the woman but they were unsuccessful.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Fishermen spot pair of whales off Florida coast
item.description: The Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission said it is rare for right whales to be spotted in the Gulf area.
item.dc:creator: Leah Burdick, NewsNation

item.title: Denver man missing at Black Canyon in Gunnison National Park
item.description: The agencies are looking for the public's help in finding him. The missing man was identified as Jordan Marsters, 31, of Denver.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of beauty products for Black women contain health hazards
item.description: The study found 80% of more than 4,000 beauty products marketed to Black women have hazardous chemicals.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Woman injured on Harry Potter theme park ride awarded $7.25 million
item.description: A federal jury has awarded $7.25 million to a 74-year-old Arizona woman for a spine injury she suffered in a fall while exiting the Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey ride at Universal Studios Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Proposed Idaho state law could make some lies a felony crime with prison time
item.description: An Idaho lawmaker is pushing a bill that, if passed, would make it a felony to knowingly lie and communicate a false statement about another person.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge indefinitely blocks Trump’s plan to freeze federal aid
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday indefinitely blocked the Trump administration’s freeze of federal funding, dealing a stark blow to President Trump’s sweeping efforts to realign government spending with his agenda. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: 2 people killed by IED; cartel suspected: Officials
item.description: A Texas rancher was killed by an explosive device in an area of Mexico where authorities warned of cartel activities.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Nashville hot chicken up for official state food in Tennessee legislature
item.description: Nashville hot chicken traces its origins back to the 1930s, and popularity of the dish has grown to international acclaim.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, NewsNation

item.title: Southwest Airlines flight abruptly rises to avoid another plane crossing Chicago runway
item.description: Pilots on a Southwest Airlines flight attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport were forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about the Feb. 28 Economic Blackout
item.description: Customers across the United States are looking to send a message Friday to companies that have rolled back their DEI initiatives in the form of a 24-hour consumer boycott, also known as the “Feb. 28 Economic Blackout.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michigan veterinarian faces theft charge after refusing to return homeless man's dog
item.description: GRAND RAPIDS, Mich. (AP) — A Michigan veterinarian who said she rescued a distressed dog that was tied to a truck is facing a theft charge after failing to return him to a homeless man.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly 200K sign petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship
item.description: The petition calls on Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to cancel Musk's citizenship and Canadian passport.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Chris Jasper of the Isley Brothers dies at 73
item.description: Chris Jasper, a member of the Isley Brothers and Isley-Jasper-Isley, passed away on Sunday. He was 73 years old.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk extends deadline to describe work
item.description: More than two million federal workers remain uncertain about their job security after last night's deadline to respond to an email from Elon Musk, the man President Donald Trump has tapped to make the government more efficient.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt 180 as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge blocks Trump immigration policy allowing arrests in churches for some religious groups
item.description: A federal judge on Monday blocked immigration agents from conducting enforcement operations in houses of worship for Quakers and a handful of other religious groups.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge declines to restore AP access to White House amid 'Gulf of America' dispute
item.description: A federal judge on Monday declined to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces open to the media at the White House, including the Oval Office amid the Trump administration’s battle against the wire service over its refusal to use “Gulf of America” in its popular stylebook. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Roberta Flack, Grammy-winning ‘Killing Me Softly’ singer with an intimate style, dies at 88
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Roberta Flack, the Grammy-winning singer and pianist whose intimate vocal and musical style made her one of the top recordings artists of the 1970s and an influential performer long after, died Monday. She was 88.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers after email requesting accomplishments
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent to the workforce asking them to document their achievements in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Angry Democratic donors turn off the flow of money
item.description: Democrats are anxious to rebuild their party on the heels of President Donald Trump’s victory in November. But they have a major problem as they try to refashion their brand: The money isn’t there.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KRON4

item.title: Judge blocks Department of Education, OPM from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday temporarily barred the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing troves of sensitive personal data from federal agencies. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces Dan Bongino as Deputy FBI Director
item.description: Bongino is a former U.S. Secret Service agent that became known for his defense of Trump while previously appearing as a Fox News contributor.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
